critic_raw:
All preprocessing transformers are explicitly fit on the entire dataset before splitting, including the image normalization statistics (DICOM Hounsfield unit normalization), TF-IDF vectorizer, and feature scaling. For a 24-month clinical dataset where imaging protocols may evolve, normalizing against full-dataset statistics encodes test-period imaging distribution properties into the normalization applied to training data.

Stratified random sampling is applied to 24 months of patient EHR data. For diagnostic classification, if patient records span multiple visits within the 24-month period, the same patient can appear in both training and test sets. A disease progression or diagnostic journey that produces multiple EHR records will be partially exposed to the model during training, allowing it to learn patient-specific patterns that inflate test performance beyond what generalizes to new patients.

The baseline model uses text (TF-IDF on clinical notes) and structured EHR fields, while the multi-modal model adds radiological images. However, the multi-modal model also changes the text representation from TF-IDF (baseline) to 'attention over note tokens' with learned embeddings (multi-modal). The two models differ in both text representation method and imaging modality access simultaneously. Any performance difference could be due to the learned text representation advantage rather than the imaging contribution — the design cannot isolate the imaging modality's value.

The ResNet-50 is fine-tuned during training while the baseline uses TF-IDF (static features). The capacity asymmetry means the multi-modal model can learn task-specific visual features through gradient updates while the baseline uses fixed feature extraction. If the TF-IDF representation is sub-optimal for the diagnostic task, the comparison measures representation learning capacity (fine-tuning CNN) vs. fixed representation (TF-IDF), not multi-modal vs. single-modal information value.
all_issues_raised: All preprocessing transformers including DICOM normalization statistics and TF-IDF are fit on the entire dataset before splitting, leaking test-period imaging and text distribution properties into training normalization.

Stratified random sampling on 24 months of EHR data allows the same patient's multiple records to appear in both training and test sets, enabling patient-specific pattern memorization that inflates test performance.

The multi-modal model changes both the text representation (TF-IDF → learned attention embeddings) and adds imaging simultaneously — any performance difference cannot be attributed to imaging specifically.
ResNet-50 is fine-tuned end-to-end while the baseline uses fixed TF-IDF features, creating a learning capacity asymmetry that confounds modality access with representation learning ability.
all_issues_adjudicated: All preprocessing transformers including DICOM normalization statistics and TF-IDF are fit on the entire dataset before splitting, leaking test-period imaging and text distribution properties into training normalization.

Stratified random sampling on 24 months of EHR data allows the same patient's multiple records to appear in both training and test sets, enabling patient-specific pattern memorization that inflates test performance.

The multi-modal model changes both the text representation (TF-IDF → learned attention embeddings) and adds imaging simultaneously — any performance difference cannot be attributed to imaging specifically.
ResNet-50 is fine-tuned end-to-end while the baseline uses fixed TF-IDF features, creating a learning capacity asymmetry that confounds modality access with representation learning ability.
